_meta.purpose: Decomposed tool description components for DEAP genetic evolution. Each tool's description is split into swappable gene segments. Crossover recombines segments across variants; mutation replaces one segment with an alternative from its pool.
_meta.verb_hierarchy: perceive > construct > diagnose > trace > extract > try (Sapir-Whorf activation order)

analyze_data_for_flow.current_description: Measure a dataset's structural fitness for 3D spatial visualization. Decompose the data into 8 signal dimensions — column count, relationship density, geographic coordinates, temporal patterns, cardinality, dimensionality, row volume, and category diversity — then produce a quantified score distinguishing datasets that gain insight from 3D rendering versus those that flatten adequately into 2D charts.

core_action: a dataset's structural fitness for 3D spatial visualization
whether data has spatial structure invisible in 2D
a dataset's potential for 3D rendering vs flat charts
data complexity across 8 signal dimensions
purpose_frame: Decompose the data into 8 signal dimensions — column count, relationship density, geographic coordinates, temporal patterns, cardinality, dimensionality, row volume, and category diversity — then produce a quantified score distinguishing datasets that gain insight from 3D rendering versus those that flatten adequately into 2D charts.
Score datasets on 8 axes to determine if 3D visualization reveals structure that 2D charts lose.
Quantify spatial, relational, geographic, and temporal signals to predict 3D visualization ROI.
Produce a fitness score across column count, relationships, geo, temporal, cardinality, dimensionality, volume, and diversity.
trigger_phrase: Use when data needs scoring for 3D potential
Invoke to assess any dataset before choosing a visualization approach
Call before uploading to Flow to verify 3D advantage over 2D
Invoke when a user has tabular data and asks about visualization

validate_csv_for_flow.current_description: Diagnose CSV data against Flow Immersive's format requirements. Validate comma-delimited structure, decompose column types (numeric, categorical, date, geographic), and measure data quality across five dimensions: format compliance, header integrity, type consistency, network graph readiness, and geographic completeness.

core_action: CSV data against Flow Immersive's format requirements
whether CSV structure meets Flow's rendering requirements
data format readiness for Flow Immersive upload
CSV structure, types, and quality for 3D visualization
purpose_frame: Validate comma-delimited structure, decompose column types (numeric, categorical, date, geographic), and measure data quality across five dimensions: format compliance, header integrity, type consistency, network graph readiness, and geographic completeness.
Check headers, delimiters, column types, and data quality. Report issues that would degrade visualization fidelity.
Run 5 diagnostic checks: format, headers, types, network readiness, geo completeness. Return pass/fail with fixes.
Decompose the CSV into column types and measure quality across format, header, type, network, and geographic dimensions.
trigger_phrase: Use when data needs format verification before Flow upload
Invoke to check CSV readiness for 3D visualization
Call when a user asks 'is my CSV formatted correctly?'
Invoke when troubleshooting why data renders incorrectly in Flow

transform_to_network_graph.current_description: Construct Flow Immersive's network graph CSV from edge-list data. Map source-target pairs into Flow's required format: an "id" column with unique node identifiers and a "connections by id" column with pipe-delimited connected node IDs — the exact structure Flow's force-directed 3D renderer consumes.

core_action: Flow Immersive's network graph CSV from edge-list data
relationship data into Flow's node + connections format
source-target pairs into Flow's 3D-renderable network structure
edge lists into the id + pipe-delimited connections format Flow requires
purpose_frame: Map source-target pairs into Flow's required format: an 'id' column with unique node identifiers and a 'connections by id' column with pipe-delimited connected node IDs — the exact structure Flow's force-directed 3D renderer consumes.
Produce the two-column format (id, connections by id) that Flow's force-directed renderer needs from any edge-list or adjacency data.
Decompose edge lists into unique nodes, then construct Flow's required format with pipe-delimited connections per node.
Convert any from-to relationship data into Flow's id + connections CSV for 3D network rendering.
trigger_phrase: Use when data has source-target pairs or from-to relationships
Invoke to reshape edge lists into Flow's network format
Call when data contains relationships, connections, or parent-child links
Invoke for social networks, org charts, supply chains, or dependency graphs

Eliminate client-side layout lag by pre-computing all node positions. Handles 10x-100x more nodes than real-time layout.
